summary_oneline: Move parrot.embeddings/stores/rerankers concrete backends into a new ai-parrot-embeddings sibling package; keep Registries + Abstracts + dispatch maps in core; preserve all existing imports via namespace extension.

localization.role: Host top-level package. Already opts into pkgutil-style namespace extension so sibling distributions can contribute submodules under parrot.*
Host package-discovery config. Already declares namespaces = true and include = ['parrot*'] — no host-side blocker to namespace contribution.
Embeddings subsystem. 9 files; 3 concrete backends (google/huggingface/openai) move; base/registry/catalog/matryoshka/processor stay.
Singleton dispatcher. Resolves backends by importlib.import_module(f'parrot.embeddings.{model_type}') — string-based, so backends can ship from any distribution that lands at the same import path.
Abstract base for embedding backends. Stays in core (per user directive).
Dispatch table: {'huggingface', 'google', 'openai'} → class names. Stays in core; Registry depends on it at init.
Vector-store subsystem. 28 files; 6 concrete backends move; abstract, models, sub-packages (kb/, parents/, utils/) likely stay.
Dispatch table re-exporting AbstractStore + {postgres, milvus, kb, faiss_store, arango, bigquery} → class names. Stays in core.
Abstract base for vector stores. Stays in core.
Shared types consumed by rerankers/bots/handlers/manager/knowledge. Must stay in core.
PgVector concrete store. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[pgvector].
Milvus concrete store. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[milvus].
Arango concrete store. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[arango].
BigQuery concrete store. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[bigquery].
FAISS concrete store. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[faiss] (Python lib faiss-cpu stays in core deps for episodic memory).
Reranker subsystem. 6 files; 2 concrete rerankers (local, llm) move; abstract/models/factory/__init__ stay.
Module-level __getattr__ already lazy-imports LocalCrossEncoderReranker / LLMReranker. Stays in core; resolution works unchanged after move.
Factory dispatch with local imports for concrete reranker classes. Stays in core.
Local cross-encoder reranker. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[reranker-local].
LLM-based reranker. Moves → ai-parrot-embeddings[reranker-llm].

Existing extras tied to embeddings/stores/rerankers. embeddings, milvus, chroma, bigquery, arango move; pgvector currently lives under images extra (line 352) and must be extracted. all / all-fast meta-extras must re-target the new package.

Closest analog: per-backend extras, depends on ai-parrot core, uv workspace dev. Reuse build-system + project + uv.sources shape; diverge on [tool.setuptools.packages.find] to use parrot* + namespaces=true instead of parrot_tools*.

Existing precedent for satellite-to-core bridging via sys.meta_path. FEAT-201 deliberately does NOT use this pattern — no redirector needed because the satellite ships modules under the same parrot.* namespace.
HTTP handlers for vector stores. Stay in core; import supported_stores, AbstractStore, supported_embeddings — all of which stay in core.
Routing layer that wires AbstractStore + MultiStoreSearchTool.StoreType. Stays in core.
Core RAG-primitive tools that wrap stores. Stay in core (per FEAT-057 explicit decision).
AbstractBot already uses lazy local imports for stores backends + EmbeddingRegistry. Pattern preserved after the move.

localization.symbol: FEAT-057 decision: proxy modules, NOT PEP 420
localization.role: Prior precedent. Explicitly rejected PEP 420 in favor of __getattr__/meta_path proxies. FEAT-201 deliberately breaks with this precedent at the user's direction.

Newer precedent. Spec called for PEP 420 namespace package but actual implementation shipped under parrot_formdesigner.* top-level. FEAT-201 aligns with this spec's stated intent but is the first real exercise of namespace-extension in the repo.

constraints.statement: Host parrot/__init__.py already calls extend_path(__path__, __name__) (pkgutil namespace extension). To make sub-package modules from the satellite distribution discoverable, the SAME extension must be added to parrot/embeddings/__init__.py, parrot/stores/__init__.py, parrot/rerankers/__init__.py — they do not call it today.
constraints.implication: FEAT-201 must include a tiny mechanical change to each of the three core sub-package __init__.py files (one-line import + one-line assignment) so the new package's modules become reachable via the existing namespace.

constraints.statement: EmbeddingRegistry, Reranker factory, and store dispatch all resolve concrete classes by import-string (importlib.import_module / local imports) — no compile-time coupling between dispatchers and concrete backends.
constraints.implication: The split is functionally transparent at runtime; no dispatcher code in core needs to change.

constraints.statement: parrot.stores.models (Document, SearchResult, StoreConfig, DistanceStrategy) is consumed by rerankers, bots, handlers, manager, knowledge, scraper — it is a shared-types module.
constraints.implication: models.py MUST stay in core. Any concrete backend that moves will continue to import from parrot.stores.models at runtime, which works under namespace extension.

constraints.statement: faiss-cpu is a core dependency of ai-parrot (line 98 of host pyproject) because episodic memory requires FAISS as the default fallback backend.
constraints.implication: Moving the FAISSStore backend out is fine, but the faiss-cpu Python library MUST remain in core dependencies (not relegated to an extra of the new package).

constraints.statement: pgvector==0.4.1 is currently bundled inside the images extra of the host pyproject (line 352) because PgVector is used for image embeddings — there is no dedicated pgvector extra today.
constraints.implication: The new ai-parrot-embeddings[pgvector] extra must carry pgvector; the host's images extra should be amended (either drop pgvector or depend on ai-parrot-embeddings[pgvector]) — a small cross-package migration step.

constraints.statement: Meta-extras all and all-fast (lines 505 and 509 of host pyproject) reference the embeddings extra of ai-parrot.
constraints.implication: After the split, both meta-extras must be rewritten to pull from ai-parrot-embeddings[...] to preserve the legacy install one-liner UX.

constraints.statement: All existing sibling packages (ai-parrot-tools / -loaders / -pipelines / parrot-formdesigner) ship under separate top-levels (parrot_tools.*, etc.) bridged via sys.meta_path redirectors in core. The TASK-548 / FEAT-079 plan to use PEP 420 was approved but reverted to the separate-top-level pattern in implementation.
constraints.implication: FEAT-201 is the first real exercise of namespace extension in this repo. Acceptance tests must verify byte-identical imports and editable-install coexistence in a clean environment — small but real risk that should be flagged for QA.

constraints.statement: All three subsystems have active recent work (matryoshka FEAT-150, contextual embedding FEAT-127/128, PgVector metadata_filters, parent-child retrieval factory FEAT-128/133, reranker factory FEAT-133) — but no in-flight SDD spec touches packaging.
constraints.implication: No collision risk on namespace boundaries, but FEAT-201 must integrate cleanly with recent features (Matryoshka, contextual embedding) — re-test the AbstractStore.create_embedding matryoshka forwarding after the SentenceTransformer move.

enrichment.scope: Create a new sibling distribution ai-parrot-embeddings under packages/. Move the concrete backend files from parrot/embeddings/, parrot/stores/, parrot/rerankers/ into the new package, leaving Registries / Abstracts / dispatch maps / shared models / public __init__.py files in core. Use pkgutil-style namespace extension (extending the existing host opt-in) so all existing imports stay byte-identical. Introduce one extra per backend on the new package; rewrite the host's meta-extras (all, all-fast) to re-target the new package; extract pgvector from the host's images extra into its proper place.
what_new: packages/ai-parrot-embeddings/pyproject.toml — name='ai-parrot-embeddings', dependencies=['ai-parrot>=...'], per-backend extras (pgvector, milvus, arango, bigquery, faiss, chroma, openai, google, huggingface, reranker-local, reranker-llm, all)
Per-backend pytest markers in the new package's tests (mirroring per-backend extras)
Workspace registration: root pyproject.toml [tool.uv.workspace] gains the new member
what_modified: packages/ai-parrot/src/parrot/embeddings/__init__.py — add extend_path call (1 line) so satellite-supplied modules are reachable; supported_embeddings map stays
packages/ai-parrot/src/parrot/stores/__init__.py — add extend_path call; supported_stores map stays

packages/ai-parrot/pyproject.toml — remove the embeddings, milvus, chroma, arango, bigquery extras (or replace with ai-parrot-embeddings[...] deps); extract pgvector from images extra; rewrite all and all-fast meta-extras to reference ai-parrot-embeddings; keep faiss-cpu in core deps
Delete the moved files from packages/ai-parrot/src/parrot/{embeddings,stores,rerankers}/ once the new package builds and tests pass
what_untouched: Public API surface — every from parrot.{embeddings,stores,rerankers}.X import Y site stays byte-identical (the load-bearing user requirement)
EmbeddingRegistry, AbstractEmbeddingModel, AbstractStore, AbstractReranker — all stay in core unchanged
Dispatch tables (supported_embeddings, supported_stores) and rerankers/__init__ lazy __getattr__ — stay in core unchanged
Shared types: parrot.stores.models (Document, SearchResult, StoreConfig, DistanceStrategy) — stays in core
Higher-level orchestration sub-packages: parrot.stores.kb, parrot.stores.parents, parrot.stores.utils — likely stay in core (revisit in spec)
Core RAG-primitive tools: parrot.tools.vectorstoresearch, parrot.tools.multistoresearch — stay in core (per FEAT-057 explicit decision)
HTTP handlers parrot.handlers.stores — stay in core
Routing layer parrot.registry.routing — stays in core

Existing sibling packages (ai-parrot-tools, -loaders, -pipelines, parrot-formdesigner) and their parrot_<name>.* + redirector machinery — not touched; FEAT-201 introduces a new convention alongside, not a retrofit
patterns_to_follow: uv-workspace + setuptools src/ layout mirroring packages/ai-parrot-tools/ (F009)
Per-backend optional-dependencies block with an all aggregator (F009)
[tool.uv.sources] ai-parrot = { workspace = true } for editable workspace dev (F009)
Dispatch-by-import-string idiom already used by EmbeddingRegistry (F006) — no change needed
Lazy / circular-avoiding local imports for heavy backends already idiomatic in bots/handlers/manager (F012)
integration_risks: Risk: namespace extension is a first-of-its-kind in this repo (TASK-548's plan reverted to parrot_<name>.*). Mitigation: explicit pytest covering 'from parrot.stores.pgvector import PgVectorStore' both with the satellite installed and not installed (the latter must produce a clear, actionable ImportError). Evidence: F010, F011.

Risk: the host pyproject's images extra depends on pgvector (entangling vision and vector-store deps). Mitigation: extract pgvector to ai-parrot-embeddings[pgvector] and amend images to declare ai-parrot-embeddings[pgvector] as a dependency. Evidence: F008.

Risk: meta-extra UX regression — users who run pip install ai-parrot[all] must still get the full functional stack. Mitigation: rewrite all to ai-parrot[...] + ai-parrot-embeddings[all]; document the change in the release notes. Evidence: F008.

Risk: Matryoshka kwarg forwarding (TASK-1037) and contextual augmentation (FEAT-127/128) wiring through AbstractStore.create_embedding and SentenceTransformerModel — must be re-validated after the move. Mitigation: keep the existing matryoshka/contextual test suite and run it against the satellite-installed environment. Evidence: F013.

Risk: editable installs in a uv workspace where both ai-parrot and ai-parrot-embeddings contribute to the same parrot.* namespace can be tricky in IDEs / PyCharm. Mitigation: document the dev workflow (uv sync --all-packages) and verify import resolution in CI. Evidence: F010.

confidence_map.statement: The user wants the new package to host three subsystems: stores, embeddings, rerankers.
confidence_map.reasoning: Explicit user clarification: 'move stores, embeddings, rerankers to new package ai-parrot-embedding'.

confidence_map.statement: The user wants imports to stay byte-identical (e.g. from parrot.stores import ... continues to work).
confidence_map.reasoning: Explicit user question + decision: 'my decision is PEP 420 (namespace package)'.

confidence_map.statement: Host parrot/__init__.py already opts into pkgutil namespace extension via extend_path.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of the file (31 lines, line 12).

confidence_map.statement: Host pyproject already declares namespaces = true under setuptools.packages.find.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read; lines 529-532.

confidence_map.statement: EmbeddingRegistry resolves backends by import-string (importlib.import_module(f'parrot.embeddings.{model_type}')) — no compile-time coupling to concrete classes.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of registry.py:149-178.

confidence_map.statement: parrot.stores follows the same string-dispatch idiom via supported_stores in __init__.py.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of stores/__init__.py:1-10.

confidence_map.statement: parrot.rerankers uses module-level __getattr__ for lazy backend loading.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of rerankers/__init__.py:30-50 and factory.py:54-83.

confidence_map.reasoning: Direct tree enumeration.

confidence_map.statement: Stores subsystem has 6 concrete top-level backends (postgres, milvus, arango, bigquery, faiss_store, pgvector) and 3 sub-packages (kb/, parents/, utils/) plus abstract, models, empty, cache.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct tree enumeration; backend class names confirmed by grep (F004 citations).

confidence_map.statement: Sub-packages parrot.stores.kb, .parents, .utils stay in core (higher-level orchestration, not backend-specific).
confidence_map.reasoning: Strong signal from imports (parents/__init__ depends on abstract; kb/store.py imports EmbeddingRegistry; utils/ are chunking helpers) — but the boundary deserves explicit confirmation at spec time, since some might arguably move.

confidence_map.statement: Rerankers subsystem has 2 concrete backends (local, llm) and 4 base files (abstract, models, factory + __init__).
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct tree enumeration + read of __init__/factory.

confidence_map.statement: parrot.stores.models (Document, SearchResult, StoreConfig, DistanceStrategy) is consumed across rerankers, bots, handlers, manager, knowledge, scraper — must stay in core.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct grep evidence — multiple cross-subsystem imports.

confidence_map.statement: faiss-cpu must remain a core dependency (episodic memory) even though FAISSStore moves out.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of host pyproject lines 96-99 with explicit code comment.

confidence_map.statement: pgvector currently lives inside the images extra (line 352 of host pyproject), not its own extra.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of host pyproject.

confidence_map.statement: Existing sibling packages (ai-parrot-tools/loaders/pipelines/formdesigner) all ship under separate top-levels (parrot_<name>.*) bridged by sys.meta_path redirectors in core — never under shared parrot.* namespace.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct reads of parrot/tools/__init__.py:1-65, parrot/loaders/__init__.py:1-16, parrot-formdesigner/src/parrot_formdesigner/__init__.py, and ai-parrot-tools/pyproject.toml include-pattern.

confidence_map.statement: FEAT-079 (formdesigner) approved spec called for PEP 420 but actual implementation reverted to parrot_formdesigner.* — FEAT-201 is the first real exercise of namespace extension in this repo.
confidence_map.reasoning: Cross-reference of spec (lines 371-384) vs actual layout (parrot_formdesigner/__init__.py exists).

confidence_map.statement: ai-parrot-tools is the closest precedent for per-backend extras + workspace shape and will be reused (except for the namespace-discovery clause).
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of ai-parrot-tools/pyproject.toml structure.

confidence_map.statement: To make satellite modules discoverable at the sub-package level, parrot.{embeddings,stores,rerankers}/__init__.py must each call extend_path(__path__, __name__) — currently none do.
confidence_map.reasoning: F001 confirms top-level extension; sub-packages were read (no extend_path lines) but the precise mechanics of how namespace extension propagates without explicit calls at sub-package level should be verified at spec/implementation time. An alternative implementation (omit __init__.py in the satellite, rely on host's existing __init__.py + extend_path at root) may also work depending on setuptools wheel layout — needs empirical check.

confidence_map.statement: Three subsystems have active recent work but no in-flight SDD spec touches packaging — no merge collision.
confidence_map.reasoning: git log shows Matryoshka, contextual embedding, PgVector metadata, parent-child retrieval, reranker factory — all about runtime behavior, not packaging.

confidence_map.statement: Three meta-extras (all, all-fast) currently reference embeddings; they will need rewrites after the split.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of host pyproject lines 503-510.

unknowns.question: Confirm the package name: ai-parrot-embeddings (plural, matches sibling convention) or ai-parrot-embedding (singular, as in the user's clarification)?
plausible_answers: ai-parrot-embedding (singular — as literally typed in the clarification)
unknowns.user_answer: ai-parrot-embeddings (plural) — matches sibling convention. Singular form in clarification treated as typo.

unknowns.question: Should the sub-packages parrot.stores.kb, .parents, .utils stay in core or move to the new package?
plausible_answers: All three stay in core (they are higher-level orchestration over AbstractStore; safest default)
parrot.stores.kb moves (it's specifically the knowledge-base abstraction) and parents + utils stay
All three move (treat parrot.stores as a single moving unit, accepting a slightly larger satellite)
unknowns.user_answer: All three stay in core. Rationale: higher-level orchestration over AbstractStore; keeps satellite minimal.

unknowns.question: Should the satellite distribution include its own src/parrot/__init__.py + src/parrot/{embeddings,stores,rerankers}/__init__.py (each calling extend_path), or omit __init__.py files entirely (pure PEP 420) and rely on the host's existing __init__.py for any package-level setup?
plausible_answers: Omit __init__.py at parrot/, parrot/embeddings/, parrot/stores/, parrot/rerankers/ in the satellite (purest PEP 420; host owns the __init__.py)
Provide minimal extend_path stubs at every level (defensive; ensures discovery under both pkgutil and PEP 420 import paths)
Hybrid: omit at parrot/ (let host's __init__ extend), provide extend_path stubs at the three sub-package levels
unknowns.user_answer: Pure PEP 420 — omit __init__.py at all four levels in the satellite. Host retains existing __init__.py. Spec must add a wheel-content test asserting absence.

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence across 14 findings, the scope is well-bounded (3 subsystems, ~11 backend files, known extras to redistribute), and the user has already resolved the load-bearing architectural choices (3-subsystem scope + namespace-extension strategy). Remaining unknowns are implementation details that fit in §8 of a spec.

meta.notes: Two user clarifications during the plan-gate phase resolved what would have been the largest unknowns (subsystem scope + namespace strategy). The remaining U1-U3 are details the spec phase should pin down. C10 and C18 are explicitly medium because the spec's empirical check (does namespace extension propagate cleanly without sub-package extend_path calls?) will resolve them.
